Package org.eventb.internal.core.ast.datatype

Class DatatypeBuilder

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.internal.core.ast.datatype.DatatypeBuilder
All Implemented Interfaces:
IDatatypeBuilder
public final class DatatypeBuilder extends Object implements IDatatypeBuilder
The datatype builder provides a way to build a new datatype. The datatype builder is first built with its string name and all its type parameters. Then some constructors could be added until the datatype finalization caused by IDatatypeBuilder.finalizeDatatype().

To ensure uniqueness of datatype objects, we maintain a registry of known datatypes and take care of not leaking copies of already known datatypes.

  • Constructor Details

    • DatatypeBuilder

      public DatatypeBuilder(FormulaFactory ff, String name, List<GivenType> params, Object origin)
      Initialize a datatype builder with a formula factory, a datatype name and the list of type parameters as given types.
      Parameters:
      ff - the initial formula factory used to build the datatype
      name - the name of the datatype
      params - the type parameters of the datatype
      origin - the origin of the datatype, or null
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given datatype name is not a valid identifier in the given factory
      IllegalArgumentException - if a type parameter was not created by the given factory
      IllegalArgumentException - if one of the type parameters has the same name as the datatype or another type parameter

  • Method Details

    • hasBasicConstructor

      public boolean hasBasicConstructor()
      Description copied from interface: IDatatypeBuilder
      Tells if the datatype has a basic constructor. A basic constructor is a constructor that does not reference the datatype itself.
      Specified by:
      hasBasicConstructor in interface IDatatypeBuilder
      Returns:
      true iff a basic constructor has already been defined

    • addConstructor

      public IConstructorBuilder addConstructor(String consName)
      Description copied from interface: IDatatypeBuilder
      Adds a new constructor to the datatype. The arguments of the constructor are then defined through the returned object. The constructor name must be an identifier name different from all names already declared in the datatype (i.e., the datatype name, the name of already added constructors and destructors). However, the given name can be the same as one of the formal type parameters of the datatype.
      Specified by:
      addConstructor in interface IDatatypeBuilder
      Parameters:
      consName - the constructor name
      Returns:
      an IConstructorBuilder for which arguments could be defined

    • parseType

      public IParseResult parseType(String strType)
      Description copied from interface: IDatatypeBuilder
      Provides a parser that can parse a string representation of a type referencing type parameters or the datatype type itself. It returns the correct intermediate representation necessary to add constructor arguments with IConstructorBuilder.addArgument(Type) or IConstructorBuilder.addArgument(String, Type).

      A datatype type must only be written as DT(X,Y,Z) with DT the datatype name and (X,Y,Z) the type parameters in the exact same order they were declared in the datatype (X first type parameter, etc.).

      If the parse result was correctly parsed (i.e. not IResult.hasProblem()) then a call to IParseResult.getParsedType() will provide a type in the correct intermediate representation of type that must be used for constructor arguments.

      Specified by:
      parseType in interface IDatatypeBuilder
      Parameters:
      strType - the string representation of a constructor argument type
      Returns:
      the parse result in the correct intermediate representation for constructor argument type
      See Also:
      • IConstructorBuilder.addArgument(Type)
      • IConstructorBuilder.addArgument(String, Type)

    • getTypeParameters

      public GivenType[] getTypeParameters()

    • getName

      public String getName()

    • asGivenType

      public GivenType asGivenType()

    • getArgumentTypeChecker

      public ArgumentTypeChecker getArgumentTypeChecker()

    • getConstructors

      public List<ConstructorBuilder> getConstructors()

    • finalizeDatatype

      public Datatype finalizeDatatype()
      Description copied from interface: IDatatypeBuilder
      Finalizes this builder and returns the assembled datatype.

      At least one basic constructor must have been added to this builder.

      Upon return from this method, this datatype builder and all constructor builders that it created are finalized and cannot be used anymore.

      Any subsequent call will return the same datatype object as the first successful call.

      Specified by:
      finalizeDatatype in interface IDatatypeBuilder
      See Also:
      • IDatatypeBuilder.hasBasicConstructor()

    • getBaseFactory

      public FormulaFactory getBaseFactory()

    • getFactory

      public FormulaFactory getFactory()
      Description copied from interface: IDatatypeBuilder
      Returns the base formula factory of this builder, that is the formula factory that created this instance.
      Specified by:
      getFactory in interface IDatatypeBuilder
      Returns:
      the base formula factory

    • getOrigin

      public Object getOrigin()
      Returns the origin of the datatype.
      Returns:
      an Object, or null
